text:
Yamanezumi (ヤマネズミ, Yamanezumi) is one of the Heavens Arena's Floor Masters. He only appears in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission 

Appearance

Yamanezumi is very thin and small, with claw-like hands and feet, which are large compared to the rest of his body. He also possesses sharp, jagged teeth; the front two resemble that of a rodent. His blonde hair spikes up into two large, separated tufts, likely meant to look like large rodent ears. His hairline comes down significantly on his forehead to form an arrow pointing between his brows. 

His only outfit shown in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission is a chartreuse jumpsuit with cut-off sleeves and shorts. He wears a belt around his waist and bands around his wrists and ankles. He is never seen wearing shoes. 

Plot

The Last Mission

Yamanezumi is first seen in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission entering the arena at Heavens Arena next to Gama while Cocco introduces the Battle Olympia tournament and the Floor Masters. 

Later, Yamanezumi is seen in a training room with the other Floor Masters, awaiting the start of the Battle Olympia fights. He busies himself with rapidly punching a punching bag. 

As Jed enters the training room, Yamanezumi is seen with some of the other Floor Masters, who are agitated by Jed's sudden intrusion. Yamanezumi and the other Floor Masters are then swiftly knocked out by Jed and injected with his blood. 

Sometime later, Gon and Killua get trapped in a room with Yamanezumi and eight of the other Floor Masters, who begin attacking the boys. Yamanezumi isn't actually shown in Gon and Killua's fight with the Floor Masters, but does appear shambling after them with the other Floor Masters as the boys make their escape. 

Abilities & Powers

 training]]

Not much is known about Yamanezumi's abilities, even less so than some of the other Floor Masters. He is not shown to have a page in Killua's magazine detailing some of the Floor Masters' abilities. However, because he is a Floor Master, it can be assumed that Yamanezumi is strong and proficient in Nen.

While training in a training room before the Battle Olympia matches start, Yamanezumi is seen rapidly punching a punching bag, which may mean he favors punching in battle.

Trivia

Yamanezumi's name means "dormouse" in Japanese.
